test.name: Locality guide for Pennsylvania industrial towns, late 1800s — steel and coal occupational records
test.description: Pennsylvania late-1800s industrial occupational guide covering BOTH the anthracite coal region (Schuylkill/Luzerne/Lackawanna: employment cards, accident reports, colliery payrolls, unions) and the Pittsburgh steel industry (employment registers, union lists, city directories), with volume_search showing coverage varying by county/access level. (Merged from former ut_014 + ut_018.)

input.user_message: I'm researching ancestors who worked in Pennsylvania's industrial towns in the late 1800s — both the steel mills around Pittsburgh and the anthracite coal-mining region (Schuylkill, Luzerne, and Lackawanna counties). What records and repositories would help trace these working families through occupational and industrial sources?

judge_context: Must mention coal mining industry context. Must highlight employment registers, union rolls, census, and church records. Must not suggest modern HR databases or statewide vital registration as the main source.

volume_search returns 23 total results (paginated, nextPageToken present) spanning all three named counties: a 95%-indexed mine worker employment card volume in Schuylkill, and browse-only mine accident reports (Luzerne) and colliery payroll ledgers (Lackawanna). The skill should reflect that digitized coverage varies by county and access level rather than treating the multi-county region as a single uniform source.

The guide must also cover the Pittsburgh-area steel industry: steel-mill employment registers, union lists, city directories, census schedules, and tax rolls, with repositories such as the Heinz History Center, Pennsylvania State Archives, and Carnegie Library named alongside the coal-region ones. Avoid modern employment/HR databases or statewide vital registration as the main occupational source.
